name: Markus Buehler
why_relevant: Created MechGPT — LLMs for mechanical reasoning and materials. Closest match to the AI-for-MechE thesis. MIT faculty.

public_info.role: McAfee Professor of Engineering (Institute-wide Endowed Chair)

name: Daniel Ritchie
why_relevant: ShapeCoder, ShapeAssembly — shape program synthesis. Very active, approachable. Brown University.

name: Adriana Schulz
why_relevant: AutoMate assembly dataset, parametric joints. Moved to Brown (fall 2025). CAD joints + assembly dataset creator.

name: Jiajun Wu
why_relevant: Stanford faculty, 3D shape programs + neural program synthesis. Co-authored LEGO manual-to-machine-plan translation paper (ECCV 2022). Co-advisor of Kenny Jones.

name: Wojciech Matusik
why_relevant: Fusion 360 dataset co-creator, computational design + fabrication. MIT CSAIL.

public_info.role: Cadence Design Systems Professor of EECS
public_info.org: MIT CSAIL — Computational Design & Fabrication Group

name: Niloy J. Mitra
why_relevant: Free2CAD, ShapeCoder, shape abstraction. UCL + Adobe Research London. Geometry + synthesis intersection.

public_info.role: Professor of Geometry Processing; Research Scientist

name: Yasutaka Furukawa
why_relevant: BrepGen (SIGGRAPH 2024) — diffusion model for B-rep CAD generation. Leading CAD generation researcher, multiple top papers. SFU + Wayve.

name: Evangelos Kalogerakis
why_relevant: CSGNet, ParSeNet — CSG program learning and parametric surface fitting. UMass Amherst.

public_info.org: Manning College of Information & Computer Sciences, UMass Amherst

name: Karl Willis
why_relevant: Senior Research Manager at Autodesk AI Lab. Led Fusion 360 Gallery Dataset, co-created BRepNet. Key CAD dataset creator — pitch CAD Arena benchmark inclusion.

public_info.role: Senior Research Manager, Autodesk AI Lab

name: Pradeep Kumar Jayaraman
why_relevant: BRepNet, SolidGen — B-rep learning and autoregressive B-rep synthesis. Autodesk Research Toronto.

name: Xiang (Sam) Xu
why_relevant: Lead author BrepGen (SIGGRAPH 2024) and SkexGen — most active 2024 CAD generation researcher. Now at Autodesk AI Lab.

name: Changjian Li
why_relevant: Sketch2CAD, CADTalk, Free2CAD. Lecturer at University of Edinburgh. Very active in CAD + sketch-based modeling.

public_info.org: School of Informatics, University of Edinburgh

name: Daniele Panozzo
why_relevant: ABC Dataset (1M+ CAD models), MeshGPT. NYU professor. Dataset foundational for geometric deep learning on CAD.

name: Faez Ahmed
why_relevant: MIT MechE faculty. LInK linkage synthesis, BikeBench engineering design benchmark. Runs DeCoDE Lab — directly overlaps with AI-for-MechE thesis.

public_info.role: Associate Professor (Doherty Chair in Ocean Utilization)
public_info.org: MIT, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering — DeCoDE Lab

name: Ken Goldberg
why_relevant: UC Berkeley EECS + IEOR. Blox-Net (VLM-driven robot assembly design from prompts), Dex-Net. Bridge between CAD generation and physical assembly.

public_info.role: Professor, EECS & IEOR; Director of AUTOLAB

name: R. Kenny Jones
why_relevant: ShapeAssembly, ShapeMOD — program-based 3D shape synthesis from parts. Postdoc at Stanford (Wu + Agrawala). PhD from Brown (Ritchie). Very active.

name: Minhyuk Sung
why_relevant: KAIST faculty. Part-level 3D shape understanding, assembly, decomposition. Formerly Adobe Research.

name: Amin Heyrani Nobari
why_relevant: LInK linkage synthesis paper, deep generative models for engineering design. MIT DeCoDE Lab (Faez Ahmed's group). Very active 2024-2025.

name: Lyle Regenwetter
why_relevant: Lead author BikeBench — engineering design benchmark with physics objectives and constraints. MIT DeCoDE Lab. Direct overlap with benchmark work.

name: Kevin Ellis
why_relevant: DreamCoder — wake-sleep program synthesis with library learning. Cornell faculty. Foundational program synthesis work, relevant to CAD program generation.

name: Siddhartha Chaudhuri
why_relevant: PartNet, assembly-based shape synthesis, probabilistic 3D shape generation. Adobe Research + IIT Bombay. Foundational work on part-based CAD.

public_info.role: Senior Research Scientist, Creative Intelligence Lab
public_info.org: Adobe Research, New York (adjunct IIT Bombay)

name: Vladimir Kim
why_relevant: Director of Applied AI/ML at Adobe. COALESCE, ComplementMe — part-based 3D assembly. Shape correspondence + assembly at Adobe Research.

public_info.role: Director of Applied AI/ML, Firefly Foundry

name: Gopal Sharma
why_relevant: CSGNet (CVPR 2018), ParSeNet — learning CSG and parametric surface representations. Now at Samsung Research. PhD UMass Amherst.

name: Angel X. Chang
why_relevant: Scan2CAD, Text2Shape — text-to-3D pioneer. SFU faculty. Canada CIFAR AI Chair. Note: Twitter account is protected.

public_info.org: Simon Fraser University, School of Computing Science

name: Jessie Frazelle
why_relevant: Co-founder & CEO of Zoo (KittyCAD) — already integrated in CAD Arena. 131K Twitter followers. Reach out to formalize partnership + co-marketing.

name: Zach Dive
why_relevant: CEO of Adam AI (YC W25) — text-to-CAD copilot, $4.1M seed. Called 'the v0 of CAD' by Guillermo Rauch. High-upside cold outreach, young team, moves fast.

name: Lin Kayser
why_relevant: Founder of Hyperganic (AI generative design for additive mfg, $13M Series A) and LEAP 71 (AI-native engineering for rockets/heat exchangers). Two relevant companies.

name: Edward Mehr
why_relevant: Co-Founder & CEO of Machina Labs — AI-guided robotic metal forming from CAD files. Ex-SpaceX/Relativity. Bridge between CAD generation and physical manufacturing.

name: Bradley Rothenberg
why_relevant: Co-Founder & CEO of nTop — implicit modeling / computational engineering. Aerospace, medical, automotive. Interesting benchmark comparison (implicit vs. B-rep).

name: Andreas Edesberg
why_relevant: CEO of Sloyd — AI parametric 3D generation for game assets, backed by a16z + Autodesk. Different approach (procedural/editable) — interesting CAD Arena comparison.

name: Simon Song Yachen
why_relevant: Founder of Tripo AI — text/image-to-3D at 6.5M users, ~$1M/month revenue. API-first, multi-vertical. Easy potential benchmark integration.

name: Jun-Yan Zhu
why_relevant: PI of BrickGPT / LegoGPT at CMU (repo already cloned). pix2pix creator, very active on X. BrickGPT won Best Paper (Marr Prize) at ICCV 2025. Will reshare relevant LEGO AI work.

name: Ava Pun
why_relevant: Lead author of BrickGPT (ICCV 2025 Best Paper). PhD student at CMU. The person who built the thing we cloned. High value cold outreach — early-career, would engage.

name: Jiahuan Pei
why_relevant: LEGO Co-builder and LEGO-MRTA (ACL 2024) — VLM for fine-grained assembly guidance in mixed reality. Now assistant professor at VU Amsterdam.

name: Pablo Cesar
why_relevant: Research Lead at CWI Amsterdam. Senior figure behind LEGO-MRTA and CWI's LEGO assembly MR work. Active on Twitter. Netherlands Prize for ICT Research 2020.

public_info.role: Research Lead, Distributed & Interactive Systems; Professor

name: Graham Taylor
why_relevant: Co-supervised LEGO graph generative model paper (NeurIPS 2020 workshop). Canada CIFAR AI Chair. U of Guelph + Vector Institute — good distribution reach in Canadian ML community. Handle unconfirmed.

public_info.role: Professor of Engineering; Canada CIFAR AI Chair

name: Jaesik Park
why_relevant: SNU professor. Supervised Budget-Aware Sequential Brick Assembly paper (TMLR). Co-created Open3D library. Broadly influential in 3D AI.

name: Rylee Thompson
why_relevant: First author of 'Building LEGO Using Deep Generative Models of Graphs' (NeurIPS 2020 workshop) — earliest graph generative approach to LEGO. Now ML researcher at Huawei.

public_info.org: Huawei (previously Vector Institute / U of Guelph)
